# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

# WARNING: This template uses default service accounts and default disk encryption.
# These should be replaced with custom service accounts, CMEK, and proper
# security hardening before any production use. See Phase 2 security tasks.

# TODO Phase 2 Security Hardening:
# - Replace default service account with least-privilege custom service account
# - Add Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) for all disks
# - Restrict service account scopes (remove cloud-platform scope)
# - Enable Shielded VM features (vTPM, Secure Boot, Integrity Monitoring)
# - Add network tags and firewall rules
# - Disable external IP if not needed
# - Enable OS Login
# - Add labels for asset management and billing

imports:
  - path: compute_instance.py

resources:
  - name: web-server-instance
    type: compute_instance.py
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      machineType: e2-medium
      network: default
      subnet: default
      diskImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-12
      diskSizeGb: 20
      diskType: pd-standard
      tags:
        - http-server
        - https-server
      metadata:
        startup-script: |
          #!/bin/bash
          apt-get update
          apt-get install -y nginx
          systemctl start nginx
          systemctl enable nginx

  - name: app-server-instance
    type: compute_instance.py
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      machineType: e2-standard-2
      network: default
      subnet: default
      diskImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-12
      diskSizeGb: 50
      diskType: pd-ssd
      tags:
        - app-server
      metadata:
        startup-script: |
          #!/bin/bash
          apt-get update
          apt-get install -y default-jdk

outputs:
  - name: webServerName
    value: $(ref.web-server-instance.name)
  - name: webServerSelfLink
    value: $(ref.web-server-instance.selfLink)
  - name: appServerName
    value: $(ref.app-server-instance.name)
  - name: appServerSelfLink
    value: $(ref.app-server-instance.selfLink)
